title: Add containers-common spec and command to podman
body:
Since containers-common package is tied to specific versions
of Podman, add tools to build the package into the contrib directory
This should help other distributions to figure out which commont
package to ship.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body:
@vrothberg @lsm5 WDYT?

This script will not work with non version vendors.  

Is there a way to look at  

github.com/containers/storage v1.38.3-0.20220214113600-26c561f9a645

And get:

26c561f9a64585d9a25d340e1ae5479eca8008a1

> @vrothberg @lsm5 WDYT?
> 
> This script will not work with non version vendors.
> 
> Is there a way to look at
> 
> github.com/containers/storage v1.38.3-0.20220214113600-26c561f9a645
> 
> And get:
> 
> 26c561f9a64585d9a25d340e1ae5479eca8008a1

You don't want to set the Version: that the package claims to be to be a commit ID.  The partial commit ID in a pseudo-version, or a tag, appear to be acceptable as part of the URLs this constructs, and some shenanigans with `git ls-remote -t` should make it possible to distinguish between the two without resorting to guessing with regular expressions.

> The partial commit ID in a pseudo-version, or a tag, appear to be acceptable as part of the URLs this constructs

In particular things like `https://github.com/containers/storage/raw/26c561f9a645/storage.conf` seem to work; I have no idea if that is documented and supported.

As to what Version: / Release: to set in the generated RPM, that might need (quite?) a bit more scripting — especially to be _idiomatic_, because Go numbering uses pre-releases (`v1.38.3-0.`…) but RPM tends to prefer post-releases (`Version: v1.38.2`, `Release: 42`), or https://docs.fedoraproject.org/en-US/packaging-guidelines/Versioning/#_snapshots also explicitly requires post-releases. But I’ll fully defer to @lsm5 about these matters.
@cevich @mheon @vrothberg @Luap99 @edsantiago Could someone tell me what the end-of-file-hook is complaining about?

`contrib/containers-common/containers-common.sh` has an unwanted empty line at its end:
```console
$ cat -vET contrib/containers-common/containers-common.sh |tail -2
        storage.conf$
$
```

Happy green test buttons
Soft LGTM from me.  I'd like a couple of folks with more scripting knowledge than me (@edsantiago @nalind @cevich) to dive in here.

I don't know the use case either, and can't get anything to work ("image" seems to need a "v5"), but I agree with @cevich's suggestion to use `-e` instead of `-x`, and I like error messages and testing, so here's a starting point for a slightly more robust version of the script.  
[containers-common.sh.txt](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/8116838/containers-common.sh.txt)

I don't know how to solve the `v5` problem.

UPDATE: the `v5` problem:
```console
$ go list -m github.com/containers/image
go list -m: module github.com/containers/image: can't resolve module using the vendor directory
        (Use -mod=mod or -mod=readonly to bypass.)
```
@nalind I cold not figure out how to get ls-remote to translate the storage tag.

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
do we need the spec itself in this repo though? Is that meant for copr builds or for something else?
I added it as an example so other distros could use it.  And the update script is using it via specgen to grab the other repos.
I think we're only half-way there since other distros still have to do quite some manual work.  I would love a `make common` or something to do everything for the user.
Well each distro is likley to have additonal changes required, for example fedora will still add repos to the registries.conf file and potentially modify containers.conf.
